hooks.prompt:
Evaluate whether Claude should stop. Start with the Stop hook JSON in $ARGUMENTS, then read the transcript at transcript_path and inspect .simple/ files if they exist.

Detect harness-level gotchas only. Ignore ordinary coding bugs or incomplete implementation unless they reveal a harness/process problem.

Look for:
1. User corrections of agent behavior
2. Repeated instructions from the user
3. Pipeline failures (planner/generator/evaluator contract problems)
4. Classification errors (simple request routed incorrectly)
5. Compaction gaps or missing persisted context

Use only these categories: user-correction, repeated-instruction, pipeline-failure, classification-error, compaction-gap.
Classify findings as HIGH or MEDIUM.
If an existing entry in .simple/gotchas.md already captures the same issue with no materially new detail, treat it as already recorded.

If there are no new HIGH or MEDIUM gotchas, return exactly:
{"ok": true}

If stop_hook_active is true, return exactly:
{"ok": true}
This prevents infinite Stop-hook loops after Claude has already been told to record the gotcha.

If new gotchas exist and stop_hook_active is false, return JSON only in this shape:
{"ok": false, "reason": "Before stopping, append the following entries to .simple/gotchas.md under a ## YYYY-MM-DD header (create .simple/ and the file if needed, avoid duplicates, and do no other work):\n- [HIGH] category: description -> suggested fix\n- [MEDIUM] category: description -> suggested fix"}

Return at most 3 bullets. The reason must contain the exact markdown bullets Claude should append. Do not ask Claude to do anything except record the gotcha entries and stop.
